claims.claim_text: Corn grown in a corn-soybean rotation (vs continuous soybean) increased soil organic carbon and labile carbon pools, driven by the C-enriched corn biomass added in the corn phase.
claims.evidence_text: An increase in SOC across the sites, except Ohio-Hoytville, under CS-C compared to SS was due to increased deposition of C inputs that consists of larger amounts of C-enriched corn biomass followed by lesser amounts of highly decomposable N-rich soybean biomass.

value.raw_value_text: The SOC values were higher by 49 to 60% under both CS phases than under SS in Alabama

provenance.source_title: Gypsum, crop rotation, and cover crop impacts on soil organic carbon and biological dynamics in rainfed transitional no-till corn-soybean systems | PLOS One

claims.claim_text: Both phases of the corn-soybean rotation (including soybean following corn) increased soil organic carbon, active C, and the carbon management index relative to continuous soybean.
claims.evidence_text: CS rotation increased SOC, active C, CPI, and CMI compared to SS, but decreased SMBC and SMBC:SOC within and across sites.

provenance.source_title: Gypsum, crop rotation, and cover crop impacts on soil organic carbon and biological dynamics in rainfed transitional no-till corn-soybean systems | PLOS One
